{{Infobox character
| name = Void Sage
| siblings = {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Kelsa|Wei Shi Kelsa]]}}
| titles = {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Void Sage]]}}
| appears_in = Cradle
| introduced = [[Reaper (novel)|Reaper]]
}}

''See also: [[Void Sage Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[The Void]] [[Sage]] is a title held by [[Lindon]], a gifted and highly trained individual who has achieved the rank of [[Sage]]. His power is fundamentally rooted in his connection to the [[Void Icon]], an entity characterized by hunger and the concept of subtraction or removal. This connection allows him to utilize [[hunger madra]] as a primary source of strength and grants him authority over empty spaces, the ability to hide or remove obstacles, and the capacity to consume power. His abilities include techniques like [[black dragon's breath]] fused with [[Void authority]] and the ability to manipulate or [[construct]] pocket worlds. Despite his significant power, [[Lindon]] is described as an immature [[Sage]]. His progression involved intense meditation to align himself with the deeper meanings of his [[Icon]], a process requiring him to empty his thoughts. While he possesses immense potential and has received blessings from figures such as the [[Slumbering Wraith]], some observers note that he is not yet a true [[Dreadgod]]. He has also been associated with the protection of [[Redmoon Hall]] and is recognized by others for having looked deeper into the Void than most living people.}}

== Reaper ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[The Void]] [[Sage]] exists as a presence capable of manifesting through [[Lindon]]. During his development, [[Lindon]]'s technique eventually fuses with the authority held by this entity. [[Dross]] later acknowledges this connection by addressing [[Lindon]] as the Void [[Sage]]. While it has been established that [[Reigan Shen]] does not hold this title, there is a possibility that the Void [[Sage]] may take control of the [[labyrinth]].}}

<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">5</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Void Sage]] is a presence that can manifest through [[Lindon]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c4">''Reaper'', chapter 4</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Lindon]]'s technique fuses with the authority of the [[Void Sage]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c14">''Reaper'', chapter 14</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Dross]] addresses [[Lindon]] as [[Void Sage]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c20">''Reaper'', chapter 20</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Reigan Shen]] is not a [[Void Sage]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c23">''Reaper'', chapter 23</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Void Sage]] may take over the [[labyrinth]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c24">''Reaper'', chapter 24</ref>}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== Dreadgod ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Wei Shi Lindon Arelius]] holds the title and authority of the Void [[Sage]], a position that ensures no one can dictate his actions. During the confrontation with the [[Dreadgod]], he utilized a method to elevate [[Yerin]] so she could strike the creature, eventually commanding it to consume. This display of power caused the [[Dreadgod]] to abandon its pursuit of him as a prize. Following these events, the Void [[Sage]] traveled to reunite with [[Yerin]].}}

<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">9</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Lindon]] has authority as the [[Void Sage]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c3">''Dreadgod'', chapter 3</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|No one is going to tell the [[Void Sage]] what to do.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c3" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Void Sage]] is a title or authority held by [[Lindon]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c15">''Dreadgod'', chapter 15</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Void Sage]] has a way to take [[Yerin]] up so she can stab [[Dreadgods]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c17">''Dreadgod'', chapter 17</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Void Sage]] said '[[Consume]]'.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c18">''Dreadgod'', chapter 18</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Dreadgod]] gave up on keeping the [[Void Sage]] as a prize.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c18" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Void Sage]] is traveling to meet [[Yerin]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c19">''Dreadgod'', chapter 19</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|A [[Void Sage]] was behind [[Lindon]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c22">''Dreadgod'', chapter 22</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Void Sage]] is [[Wei Shi Lindon Arelius]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c22" />}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== Waybound ==
<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">3</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Wei Shi [[Kelsa]] is the sister of the [[Void Sage]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c6">''Waybound'', chapter 6</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|People wanted to borrow the protection of the [[Void Sage]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c32">''Waybound'', chapter 32</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Void Sage]] believes [[Reigan Shen’s Remnant]] was holding onto their inheritance.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c33">''Waybound'', chapter 33</ref>}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== Threshold: Stories from Cradle ==
<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">1</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Kelsa]] is the sister of the [[Void Sage]].<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c8">''Threshold: Stories from Cradle'', chapter 8</ref>}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|“Wei Shi Kelsa, sister of the Void, speaks with his voice.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c6" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|“I believe you were holding on to my inheritance.”<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c33" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Cradle nav}}

[[Category:Characters]]
[[Category:Reaper]]
[[Category:Dreadgod]]
[[Category:Waybound]]
[[Category:Threshold: Stories from Cradle]]
[[Category:Cradle]]
[[Category:Cradle characters]]
[[Category:Titled characters]]
